title: बनारस की चौथी रात भी रही देश में सबसे गर्म, दिन में तीसरे स्थान पर कायम - Jagran
search_term: ("पारा चढ़ा" OR "पारा पहुंचा" OR तापमान OR "तापमान बढ़ा" OR "रिकॉर्ड तोड़ गर्मी" OR "डिग्री सेल्सियस" OR "पारा उबला" OR "अधिकतम तापमान" OR "तापमान 40 पार" OR "तापमान 45 पार" OR "तापमान बढ़ने की संभावना") Uttar Pradesh
full_text:
बनारस की चौथी रात भी रही देश में सबसे गर्म, दिन में तीसरे स्थान पर कायम
बनारस में लगातार चौथी रात देश की सबसे गर्म रही, जबकि दिन में यह तीसरा सबसे गर्म शहर बना रहा। मौसम विभाग ने अगले चार-पांच दिनों तक तापमान बढ़ने और लू क ...और पढ़ें
HighLights
लगातार चौथी रात बनारस देश में सबसे गर्म रहा।
दिन में वाराणसी देश का तीसरा सबसे गर्म शहर।
अगले 4-5 दिन तापमान बढ़ने, लू चलने का अनुमान।
जागरण संवाददाता, वाराणसी। दिन तो दिन रातें भी बेचैन किए हुए हैंं। लगातार चौथे दिन बनारस की रात देश मेें सबसे गर्म रही तो दिन की गर्मी में यह बांदा, प्रयागराज के बाद देश भर में तीसरे पायदान पर बना रहा। मौसम विभाग का पूर्वानुमान है कि अभी चार-पांच दिनों तक तापमान बढ़ता ही जाएगा और उष्ण लहर की यही स्थिति बनी रहेगी।
इन परिस्थितियों में फिलहाल मानसून के आगे बढ़ने की संभावना नहीं है, स्थिति में किसी भी प्रकार के परिवर्तन का अनुमान अभी 25 जून के बाद ही लगाया जा सकता है। वैसे शनिवार को कुछ क्षेत्रों में दोपहर बाद आंधी चली और नाममात्र की बूंदाबांदी भी हुई, इससे तापमान कल की सीमा पार न कर सका और तनिक नीचे ही रह गया लेकिन रविवार से फिर तापमान बढ़ने की संभावना बनी हुई है।
शनिवार को सुबह से ही निकली प्रचंड धूप व आर्द्रता के मेल से आभासी तापमान वास्तविक तापमान से छह डिग्री सेल्सियस अधिक प्रतीत हो रहा था। उबलते भाव की तरह गर्मी दिन तो दिन, देर रात तक देह को झुलसाती रही। इस तापलहर की चपेट में हर जीव-जंतु और वनस्पति झुलस रहे हैं। अस्पताल में बीमारों की संख्या बढती जा रही है।
सुबह से हुई तीखी धूप दोपहर एक बजे तक 41 डिग्री सेल्सियस तक पहुंची तो गर्मी 47 डिग्री सेल्सियस जितनी लग रही थी। आधे घंटे बाद ही बादल दिखने लगे इससे दोपहर ढाई बजे अधिकतम तापमान बीते 24 घंटे की अपेक्षा 0.4 डिग्री सेल्सियस पहले रुक गया और सामान्य से 5.7 डिग्री सेल्सियस अधिक 42.6 डिग्री सेल्सियस पर पहुंचकर 49 डिग्री सेल्सियस जितने ताप की अनुभूूति कराता रहा।
दक्षिणी क्षेत्र में दोपहर बाद आंधी और बूंदाबांदी
साढ़े तीन बजे तक ताप की यही स्थिति बनी रही, हालांकि ठीक उसी समय बीएचयू और उससे दक्षिण के क्षेत्रों में उसी समय आंधी आई और बूंदाबांदी हुई। इसके बाद उमस भरी गर्मी और भी बढ़ गई। न्यूनतम तापमान सामान्य से 3.4 डिग्री सेल्सियस अधिक 30.8 तक पहुंचने से बनारस की चौथी रात भी पूरे देश में सबसे गर्म रही।
खबरें और भी

बादलों के आने से आर्द्रता में थोड़ी वृद्धि हुई और यह 59 से 57 प्रतिशत के बीच रहा। बाबतपुर क्षेत्र में अधिकतम तापमान एक डिग्री सेल्सियस कम होकर सामान्य से 3.6 डिग्री सेल्सियस ऊपर 41.2 रहा। उधर न्यूनतम तापमान सामान्य से 0.4 डिग्री सेल्सियस अधिक 27.8 डिग्री सेल्सियस बना रहा।
देश के सबसे गर्म पांच शहर
- 42.4° से. - खजुराहो व नागपुर

title: Delayed monsoon scorches UP: Sprinklers installed for animals in zoo; 31 cities face heatwave conditions - Bhaskar English
full_text:
Delayed monsoon scorches UP:Sprinklers installed for animals in zoo; 31 cities face heatwave conditions
Lucknow/Varanasi/Prayagraj/Kanpur8 hours ago
The delayed arrival of the monsoon has intensified heat across Uttar Pradesh. Most cities are witnessing strong sunshine on Sunday, while low wind speeds have increased humidity levels. The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has not forecast widespread rainfall, although intermittent cloud movement may bring light drizzle in some areas.
The monsoon remains stalled near the UP-Bihar border and has been stationary around Maharajganj for the past 8-9 days. According to the IMD, the system may start advancing again from June 23 and could enter Uttar Pradesh by June 25.
Animals struggle with heat in Gorakhpur and Kanpur zoos
The soaring temperatures are affecting animals as well. In Gorakhpur Zoo, a tigress spends most of the day inside a swimming pool and comes out only after temperatures fall in the evening.
In Kanpur Zoo, sprinklers have been installed for a rhinoceros to help it cope with the heat. The animal is frequently seen standing under water sprays during the day.
Heatwave alert issued for 31 districts
The IMD has issued a heatwave alert for 31 districts on Sunday. Hot winds and above-normal temperatures are likely to continue in several parts of eastern Uttar Pradesh and Bundelkhand until the monsoon arrives.
The districts under alert include Banda, Chitrakoot, Kaushambi, Prayagraj, Fatehpur, Pratapgarh, Sonbhadra, Mirzapur, Chandauli, Varanasi, Sant Ravidas Nagar, Jaunpur, Ghazipur, Azamgarh, Mau, Ballia, Gonda, Shravasti, Bahraich, Lakhimpur Kheri, Sitapur, Hardoi, Kanpur Nagar, Unnao, Lucknow, Barabanki, Rae Bareli, Amethi, Sultanpur, Ayodhya and Ambedkar Nagar.
Banda hottest in the country; rain brings brief relief in six cities
On Saturday, rainfall was recorded in six cities, including Lucknow, Hathras and Sonbhadra, providing temporary relief from the heat.
However, Banda remained the hottest place in the country, recording a maximum temperature of 43.2°C.
Severe heat likely for next 3-4 days
Lucknow-based meteorologist Atul Kumar Singh said Uttar Pradesh is likely to witness severe heat for the next three to four days. Temperatures are expected to rise further, worsening heatwave conditions.
He said the monsoon's progress remains slow and conditions are not yet favourable for its arrival in the state. According to him, people may have to wait at least five more days for monsoon relief.
Weather outlook for next five days
- June 22: No significant rainfall is expected across the state. Heatwave conditions may persist in eastern Uttar Pradesh, while temperatures could rise by one to two degrees Celsius.
- June 23: Clear weather is likely in both eastern and western Uttar Pradesh. Strong sunshine and hot winds may continue, with little chance of rain.
- June 24: Cloud cover is expected across most districts. Isolated heavy rainfall may occur in parts of eastern Uttar Pradesh, while western districts are likely to remain dry.
- June 25: Rainfall and gusty winds are expected in eastern Uttar Pradesh. The monsoon may enter the state on this day. Heatwave conditions may continue in western regions.
- June 26: Most parts of the state are likely to witness cloudy skies, strong winds and rainfall, bringing relief from the prevailing heat.
UP may receive 8% below-normal rainfall this season
Senior scientist Atul Singh said Uttar Pradesh is likely to receive below-normal rainfall this year. The state generally records 820-840 mm of rainfall between June and September. However, this year's total rainfall is projected to remain around 754-773 mm, nearly 8% below normal.
El Niño conditions may weaken monsoon
IMD Director General Dr M. Mahapatra said conditions in the Pacific Ocean are showing signs of shifting towards El Niño after the end of La Niña-like conditions.
He said this transition could reduce rainfall during the southwest monsoon season. Lower snow cover in the Northern Hemisphere between January and March may also affect monsoon performance this year.
Impact on agriculture and prices
Below-normal rainfall could affect agriculture, the rural economy and household budgets across the state. Paddy production may decline by up to 20%, while delayed rainfall in June and July could postpone transplantation and reduce the cultivated area.
Farmers may have to depend more on tube wells for irrigation, increasing electricity and diesel expenses. Lower crop output could push up prices of grains, pulses and vegetables. Drought concerns may also intensify in Bundelkhand and south-western Uttar Pradesh districts such as Jhansi, Mahoba, Lalitpur and Mathura.
Last year, UP received excess monsoon rainfall
According to the IMD, Uttar Pradesh recorded 870-900 mm of rainfall during the 2025 monsoon season, around 10-15% higher than the state's normal seasonal average.

title: Uttar Pradesh Latest Weather Update: Heatwave Alert Continues, Know When Rain May Bring Relief - Asianet Newsable
full_text:
UP Weather Update: Severe heatwave conditions continue across several cities including Lucknow, Kanpur, Prayagraj and Varanasi. Temperatures remain high, while residents are waiting for relief as the forecast hints at possible weather changes in the coming days.
The weather in Uttar Pradesh on June 21, 2026, will be a mixed bag across different districts. Many parts of Purvanchal and Central UP will continue to reel under intense heat and a heatwave. However, some districts might see partly cloudy skies and thunderstorms. According to the weather department's forecast, cities like Kanpur, Lucknow, Prayagraj, Varanasi, Banda, and Ballia could see temperatures hitting 41 to 43 degrees Celsius. Meanwhile, Agra and Gorakhpur might get some slight relief. Here’s a look at what the IMD weather forecast says for major UP cities today.
Heatwave grips Purvanchal; caution advised in Ballia and Varanasi
In Ballia, the maximum temperature is expected to be 41°C and the minimum 29°C on June 21. A heatwave warning is in place here. Varanasi is also likely to face a heatwave with temperatures around 41°C. Gorakhpur will have clear skies, but the temperature could reach 39°C. The weather department has also predicted that the heatwave's impact will increase in Gorakhpur on June 22 and 23.
No relief from the heat in Lucknow, Kanpur, and Prayagraj
There's no relief from the heat in sight for these major cities. The capital, Lucknow, could see temperatures climb to 42°C, with clear skies and heatwave conditions. Kanpur also has a heatwave warning, with the maximum temperature expected to hit 43°C. In Prayagraj, the heatwave will continue for the next four days, and the temperature is likely to stay around 43°C. People have been advised to avoid stepping out in the afternoon.
Banda to be the hottest spot in Bundelkhand
Banda will be one of the hottest districts in the state. The maximum temperature here is likely to be 43°C on June 21. The heatwave is expected to continue until June 24, after which the temperature might see a slight drop.
How will the weather be in Agra, Bareilly, and Meerut?
Agra is expected to have mainly clear weather with the temperature at 41°C. However, some light rain and thunderstorm activity might be recorded on June 20. Bareilly and Meerut will also have mostly clear skies. The maximum temperature in both districts is expected to be between 39 and 40°C, and there are no warnings for now.
Clouds to increase over Ayodhya and Basti
The weather department has indicated sunny weather for the entire week in Ayodhya. Meanwhile, Basti might see some normal cloud cover on June 21. There's a chance of light rain or drizzle with thunderstorms on June 22 and 23, which could bring the temperature down to 39°C.
UP weather trend for the next 5 days: When will relief come?
According to the weather department, the heat and heatwave conditions will persist in most parts of Uttar Pradesh until June 24. From June 25, there are signs of increasing cloud cover and some rain in parts of Eastern UP, like Gorakhpur, Ballia, and Basti. This could lead to a drop in temperature by 2 to 4 degrees Celsius.
Advisory for residents
- Take special care of children, the elderly, and those who are unwell.
- Try to avoid unnecessary travel in districts affected by the heatwave.
